intro: I'm Willian, a Fullstack Developer focused on React, Node.js, and PostgreSQL.
I build complete web applications: from API architecture and database modeling to user-friendly frontends.
My projects are production-ready — with authentication, structured database schemas, and clean deployment on Vercel and Railway.

about.title: About me
about.description: I'm a Fullstack Developer with a focus on React, Node.js, and PostgreSQL. After completing my training at Syntax Institut and passing the IHK exam, I build web applications that go beyond UI demos: structured database schemas, protected API endpoints, and clear business logic. Projects like LEGO Set Explorer and ContainerHub show how I connect frontend, backend, and database into a coherent product.

workStyle.title: Architecture first
workStyle.description: I plan components, API endpoints, and database schemas before implementing — for structured, maintainable code.

workStyle.title: Version control & collaboration
workStyle.description: Git branches, pull requests, and meaningful commit messages are standard practice for me, not optional.

workStyle.title: Product thinking
workStyle.description: I think in user workflows and requirements. UX and backend logic need to fit together — not be designed in isolation.

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Production-ready projects with real requirements — fullstack architecture, authentication, and database modeling.

list.title: LEGO Set Explorer
list.description: Built a searchable, filterable LEGO set database with personal collection management and a statistics dashboard. Full-stack application with React, Node.js/Express in TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and Docker Compose — frontend on Vercel, backend on Railway.
highlights: Development of a TypeScript REST API (Node.js/Express) with 9 endpoints: Sets, Auth, Collections, and Statistics
Implementation of session authentication with registration, login, and access control
Complex SQL queries with multi-filters (theme, year, piece count) and server-side pagination
PostgreSQL connection pooling with automatic schema initialization on startup
Statistics dashboard with aggregated SQL queries
Docker Compose locally · Vercel (frontend) + Railway (backend + PostgreSQL) in production

list.title: Heilpraktiker-Bleialf
list.description: The practice's existing website was technically outdated with no digital functionality. Full rebuild in Next.js with TypeScript: modern design, online appointment booking, patient portal with role-based authentication, and admin panel.
highlights: Implementation of JWT session authentication with bcrypt and role separation (Admin / Patient)
Appointment API with practitioner validation and status workflow (PENDING → CONFIRMED → CANCELED)
Automated emails on login, appointment request, and status change
Prisma ORM + PostgreSQL: structured schemas for Users, Appointments, and Patients
Admin panel with appointment management, availability check, and notification system
Next.js App Router · TypeScript throughout · deployed on Vercel

list.title: ContainerHub
list.description: A container rental business needed a digital inventory management platform with a clear status workflow. Built with a React frontend, Strapi as headless CMS, and PostgreSQL — complete CRUD flow with status-based data persistence, deployed on Vercel.
highlights: Integration of the Strapi REST API via /api/containers (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
Implementation of status logic: draft → active with PostgreSQL data persistence
Strapi headless CMS with configurable access and content management
Controlled React state management for dynamic status transitions
Vercel deployment with environment variables and automated build pipeline

list.title: Pokédex WebApp
list.description: Entry-level project for learning asynchronous data communication, component architecture, and state management in React. Interactive SPA with real-time data fetching from the PokéAPI, search functionality, and a modal detail view.
highlights: Implementation of asynchronous API communication using fetch() and async/await
Controlled state management for search filters, pagination, and detail view
Component architecture: App → PokémonList → PokémonCard
Responsive grid layout with dynamic modal overlay
Deployed via GitHub Pages — publicly accessible

list.title: Toytopia
list.description: Toy e-commerce UI focused on layout, components, and UX.
highlights: Product cards & filters
Cart state management
